content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 17, 2003


Neal Andrews, Councilmember
City of San Buenaventura
Post Office Box 1623
Ventura, CA 93002-1623

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-03-100

Dear Mr. Andrews:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You are a city council member for the City of Ventura. You have posed three different situations, two of which deal with the mass mailing provisions.

In the two situations described in your letter, you are requesting advice regarding the mass mailing provisions for actions that have already occurred. The Commission does not render advice when the requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (c)(4).) Therefore, we are unable to advise you on either of these issues.

QUESTIONS

You have made a donation for a city park from your personal funds.

1.) Do the mass mailing provisions of the Act prohibit you from listing your official title as a city council member along with your name on a plaque to acknowledge the donors?

2.) Do any other provisions of the Act apply to this situation?

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-03-100
Page No. 2

CONCLUSIONS

1.)     The mass mailing provisions of the Act do not apply because
the first criteria of an "item sent" is not met by the use of your official
title on a plaque for a donors' wall.

2.)     There are no provisions of the Act that would prohibit this use
of your personal funds and official title in the way described. Since
we only have jurisdiction to advise pursuant to the Act, please contact
your city attorney to ascertain if there are other laws that would apply
to this situation.

FACTS

You have made a donation from your personal funds to a private fund raising
program to support the capital development of a new city park. The city is the receipent of
the donations, even though the campaign is organized and managed by an independent
citizen group. The city must process and approve the donor wall and plaques.

Each donor may have his or her name listed on a plaque for a donor wall. You
would like to list your official title as a city council member along with your name.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 of the Act provides that "[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing shall
be sent at public expense." Under Commission regulations 18435 and 18901, a mass
mailing is defined as more than 200 substantially similar tangible items delivered in a
calendar month, by any means, to recipients at their residence, place of employment,
business, or post office box. Regulation 18901(a) states:

"(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is
prohibited by Section 89001 if all of the following criteria are
met:
     (1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the
recipient at his or her residence, place of employment or
business, or post office box. For purposes of this subdivision
(a)(1), the item delivered to the recipient must be a tangible
item, such as a videotape, record, or button, or a written
document.
     (2) The item sent either:
          (A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency
which produces or sends the mailing, or
          (B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other
reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency

File No. A-03-100
Page No. 3

which produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared or sent
in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.
(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public
moneys; or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding
$50.00 are paid with public moneys, and the design,
production, or printing is done with the intent of sending the
item other than as permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are
sent, in a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in
response to an unsolicited request and any item described in
subdivision (b)."

In order for the mass mailing provisions to apply, all four of the criteria listed above
must be met. In your situation, there is not an "item sent" pursuant to regulation
18901(a)(1). Therefore, the use of your official title as a city council member next to your
name on the donors' plaque is not prohibited by the mass mailing regulation.

You also inquired if there could be a potential violation of any other FPPC rules.
There are provisions in the Act that restrict the use of campaign funds for donations
(section 89515). However, there are no provisions in the Act that would prohibit this use
of your personal funds. Since we only have jurisdiction to advise pursuant to the Act,
please contact your city attorney to ascertain if any other laws outside the jurisdiction of
the Political Reform Act would apply to this situation.

Advice Procedure

As you have been informed, there are many ways that you may obtain advice from
the FPPC. However, the advice we provide is prospective in nature. If you need advice
immediately, you may call the FPPC (1-866-275-3772) to speak to a consultant or staff
counsel, however, this verbal advice does not render immunity. You may also request
written advice from the legal staff pursuant to regulation 18329, copy enclosed. Please
note that there is a 21 working day time period for written advice and it does provide the
requestor with immunity. (Section 83114.)

Enclosed is a copy of regulation 18901, to assist you with mass mailing issues. In
addition, we have a very informative website, found at www.fppc.ca.gov. From there you
can access an excellent booklet (under library & publications) entitled "How Do I Get
Advice from the FPPC?" If you do not have online access, we can send a copy to you.

File No. A-03-100
Page No. 4

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [illegible] Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures

I:\AdviceLtrs\03-100

cc: Bob Boehme, City Attorney
